title: CausalMixGPD: Bayesian Nonparametric Models with Optional Heavy-Tail Augmentation and Causal Extensions
text: Causal inference often summarized average effects, many applications require distributional comparisons treatment regimes. clinical trials, investigators may want understand treatment changes outcomes across outcome scale, including effects among individuals upper tail events rare consequences can substantial. shift mean-based distribution-based causal questions makes causal analysis tightly linked well can estimate (predict) outcome distributions competing treatment assignments. Dirichlet Process Mixtures (DPMs) provide flexible way model outcome distributions accommodate multimodality latent heterogeneity without fixing number mixture components advance. also induce random partitions latent allocation variables, density estimation clustering naturally linked. Bayesian cluster analysis therefore typically summarized label-invariant quantities pairwise co-clustering probabilities representative partitions (Binder 1978; Dahl 2006). regression settings, dependent Dirichlet process constructions allow mixture weights, component-specific parameters, vary predictors (MacEachern 1999, MacEachern2000; De Iorio et al. 2004; Quintana et al. 2022), predictor-dependent stick-breaking schemes provide practical route covariate-modulated cluster prevalence (Ren et al. 2011). Heavy-tailed outcomes add second challenge. model must fit center distribution well still supporting principled upper tail extrapolation data become sparse. CausalMixGPD addresses combination joining Bayesian nonparametric mixtures, optional generalized Pareto tails, clustering, causal contrasts single workflow. article introduces R package (CausalMixGPD) connects Bayesian nonparametric modeling EVT, clustering, robust causal analyses presence covariates (confounders). package provides three simultaneous capabilities: Bayesian nonparametric (BNP) density modeling prediction optional GPD tail: CausalMixGPD fits DPMs using user-chosen kernel certain threshold (bulk part), can optionally splice generalized Pareto tail threshold (tail part) enable estimation tail quantities posterior predictive inference rare-event summaries. Clustering predictor dependent mixtures: dedicated clustering interface bulk-spliced models, supporting weight dependence, parameter dependence, , returning label-invariant clustering summaries posterior similarity matrices Dahl representative partitions (Binder 1978; Dahl 2006). Causal estimation prediction randomized controlled trials (RCTs) observational studies: final goal package provide causal estimands modeling treatment-specific potential outcome densities reporting contrasts functionals posterior densities. Framework supports randomized observational settings, latter using flexible Bayesian propensity score model adjust confounding. Overall, CausalMixGPD offers unified workflow density estimation, prediction, causal reporting tail behavior significance. package implemented R computationally intensive components coded C++ integrated via nimble framework model specification posterior simulation (nimble 2025). remainder article organized follows. Background Preliminaries section introduces key modeling components, including DPMs, GPDs, spliced bulk-tail construction. Package Overview section describes package’s main functions user interface. two Data analysis sections illustrate clustering causal inference workflows R datasets. Finally, Discussion section concludes limitations future directions.

what: Augmented DPM–GPD model, likelihood, and priors
title: CausalMixGPD: Bayesian Nonparametric Models with Optional Heavy-Tail Augmentation and Causal Extensions
text: Let observed data 𝒟={(yi,xi)}=1n,   \mathcal{D}=\{(y_i,x_i)\}_{=1}^n, xi∈ℝpx_i\\mathbb{R}^p denoting covariate vector (including intercept desired). target conditional distribution F(⋅∣x)F(\cdot\mid x) spliced DPM–GPD construction defined Background Preliminaries section, spliced CDF density given Background Preliminaries section. Posterior computation CausalMixGPD approximates bulk DPM finite truncation JJ mixture components, fDP(y∣x;Θ)≈∑j=1Jwjk(y∣x;θj),j=1,…,J,   f_{\mathrm{DP}}(y\mid x;\Theta)\;\approx\;\sum_{j=1}^{J} w_j\,k(y\mid x;\theta_j),   \qquad j=1,\ldots,J,  jj indexes mixture components, wj≥0w_j\ge 0, ∑j=1Jwj=1\sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. Kernel parameters can constant covariate-dependent link specifications form g(ψj(x))=x⊤βj,ψ,   g\!\left(\psi_j(x)\right)=x^\top \beta_{j,\psi}, g(⋅)g(\cdot) enforces parameter support (e.g., positive scale parameters). spliced model, let u(x)u(x) threshold, σ(x)>0\sigma(x)>0 tail scale, ξ∈ℝ\xi\\mathbb{R} tail shape (spliced-model subsection). Define δi=𝟏{yi>u(xi)}\delta_i=\mathbf{1}\{y_i>u(x_i)\}. Using spliced density truncated bulk mixture, likelihood L(Θ,Φ;𝒟)=∏=1nf(yi∣xi;Θ,Φ),   L(\Theta,\Phi;\mathcal{D})   =\prod_{=1}^n f(y_i\mid x_i;\Theta,\Phi),  Θ\Theta collects truncation-level bulk parameters {wj,θj}j=1J\{w_j,\theta_j\}_{j=1}^J, Φ\Phi collects tail parameters governing u(x)u(x), σ(x)\sigma(x), ξ\xi. Equivalently, log-likelihood can written $$   \log L(\Theta,\Phi;\mathcal{D})   \;=&\;\sum_{=1}^n (1-\delta_i)\,\log f_{\mathrm{DP}}(y_i\mid x_i;\Theta)   \nonumber\\   &\;+\;\sum_{=1}^n \delta_i   \Bigl[     \log\!\bigl\{1-F_{\mathrm{DP}}(u(x_i)\mid x_i;\Theta)\bigr\}     +\log f_{\mathrm{GPD}}\!\bigl(y_i\mid x_i;\Phi\bigr)   \Bigr], $$ fGPDf_{\mathrm{GPD}} GPD density FDP(⋅∣x)F_{\mathrm{DP}}(\cdot\mid x) bulk mixture CDF induced truncated mixture. Priors follow decomposition implied bulk-tail factorization. SB backend, truncation-level weights generated Beta stick breaks stick-breaking construction, represented nimble using Beta distribution model code (see dbeta). CRP backend, latent-cluster-label representation implemented using CRP distribution nimble (see dCRP), matching predictive representation CRP. Kernel parameters tail parameters assigned weakly informative priors, regression coefficients βj,ψ\beta_{j,\psi} arising link representation described . link construction used covariate-dependent u(x)u(x) σ(x)\sigma(x) modeled functions xx.
code: data("nc_posX100_p3_k2", package = "CausalMixGPD") overview_dat <- data.frame(y = nc_posX100_p3_k2$y, nc_posX100_p3_k2$X) mcmc_fixed overview_fit <- dpmgpd(   formula = y ~ x1 + x2 + x3,   data = overview_dat,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "lognormal",   components = 5,   mcmc = mcmc_fixed )

Posterior summaries intervals formed {μ(s)(x)}s=1S\{\mu^{(s)}(x)\}_{s=1}^S {μc(s)(x)}s=1S\{\mu_c^{(s)}(x)\}_{s=1}^S. spliced model, conditional mean infinite ξ≥1\xi\ge 1; accordingly, CausalMixGPD flags type = "mean" infinite posterior mass ξ≥1\xi\ge 1, type = "rmean" remains finite construction. Using fitted model overview_fit Augmented DPM–GPD model subsection, now compute posterior summaries original training covariates (default behavior predict() newdata supplied). new covariate value x⋆x^\star, prediction based posterior predictive distribution p(y⋆∣x⋆,𝒟)=∫f(y⋆∣x⋆;Θ,Φ)p(Θ,Φ∣𝒟)dΘdΦ.   p(y^\star\mid x^\star,\mathcal{D})   =\int f(y^\star\mid x^\star;\Theta,\Phi)\,p(\Theta,\Phi\mid \mathcal{D})\,d\Theta\,d\Phi.  predict() method returns posterior summaries functional targets current posterior-functionals subsection. type = "density" type = "survival", user supplies grid yy values method returns pointwise posterior means interval bounds computed draw--draw. type = "quantile", user supplies quantile indices τ\tau method returns posterior summaries Q(τ∣x⋆)Q(\tau\mid x^\star) computed using spliced quantile representation . type = "mean" type = "rmean", prediction uses posterior predictive Monte Carlo described , xx replaced x⋆x^\star user-controlled MM nsim_mean.
code: overview_q_grid <- c(0.50, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99) overview_x_eval <- overview_dat[1:20, c("x1", "x2", "x3")]  overview_est_quant <- predict(   overview_fit,   newdata = overview_x_eval,   type = "quantile",   index = overview_q_grid,   interval = "hpd",   level = 0.95 ) overview_q_levels <- c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75) overview_x_new <- as.data.frame(lapply(   overview_dat[, c("x1", "x2", "x3")],   quantile,   probs = overview_q_levels,   na.rm = TRUE )) rownames(overview_x_new) <- c("q25", "q50", "q75") overview_y_grid <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 200) overview_p_grid <- c(0.50, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99) overview_pdens <- predict(   overview_fit,   newdata = overview_x_new,   y = overview_y_grid,   type = "density",   interval = "credible",   level = 0.95 ) overview_psurv <- predict(   overview_fit,   newdata = overview_x_new,   y = overview_y_grid,   type = "survival",   interval = "credible",   level = 0.95 ) overview_pquant <- predict(   overview_fit,   newdata = overview_x_new,   type = "quantile",   index = overview_p_grid,   interval = "hpd",   level = 0.95 )

Users specify response variable, optional covariates, mixture kernel, whether model use bulk-spliced likelihood. resulting model fitted using MCMC framework used conditional causal models. Consequently, clustering models share prior specification, kernel choices, computational infrastructure rest package. Two clustering wrappers provided. first constructs mixture models using bulk kernel distribution. second uses spliced bulk–tail likelihood introduced earlier, allowing clustering structure account extreme observations generalized Pareto tail. interfaces accept model specification arguments main modeling functions, ensuring clustering models can constructed using syntax options. Three forms covariate dependence supported clustering models. First, covariates may influence mixture weights component distributions remain invariant across observations. configuration, clusters represent subpopulations whose prevalence varies predictors. Second, mixture weights may remain global component parameters depend covariates link structures used conditional density models. case clusters represent groups distinct covariate-response relationships. Third, mixture weights component parameters may depend covariates simultaneously, allowing clusters capture changing prevalence covariate-dependent distributional behavior. Posterior inference clustering models uses sampling procedure main mixture models, jointly updating component parameters latent cluster labels. Posterior clustering summaries can obtained directly fitted object. following code returns PSM, representative training partition, training cluster sizes, associated certainty scores. Cluster assignments new observations obtained posterior predictive score matrix interface. workflow applies spliced mixture model, incorporates generalized Pareto tail component clustering data heavy-tailed behavior.
code: data("nc_realX100_p3_k2", package = "CausalMixGPD")  dat_cl <- data.frame(y = nc_realX100_p3_k2$y, nc_realX100_p3_k2$X)  fit_cluster <- dpmix.cluster(   y ~ x1 + x2 + x3,   data       = dat_cl,   kernel     = "normal",   type       = "param",   components = 8,   mcmc = mcmc_fixed ) cluster_psm   <- predict(fit_cluster, type = "psm") cluster_train <- predict(fit_cluster, type = "label", return_scores = TRUE)  cluster_sizes <- data.frame(   cluster = names(summary(cluster_train)$cluster_sizes),   n = as.integer(summary(cluster_train)$cluster_sizes) ) cluster_certainty <- apply(cluster_train$scores, 1, max) cluster_new <- predict(   fit_cluster,   newdata = dat_cl,   type    = "label",   return_scores = TRUE )  cluster_new_certainty <- apply(cluster_new$scores, 1, max) fit_cluster_spliced <- dpmgpd.cluster(   y ~ x1 + x2 + x3,   data       = dat_cl,   kernel     = "normal",   type       = "both",   components = 8,   mcmc = mcmc_fixed )

title: CausalMixGPD: Bayesian Nonparametric Models with Optional Heavy-Tail Augmentation and Causal Extensions
text: following example uses formula interface high-level wrapper, fitting augmented spliced model default. fitted model object overview_causal_fit contains MCMC samples PS arm-specific outcome models, can used compute causal estimands predictive summaries. estimate treated-standardized mean quantile contrasts, use att() qtt() functions together summary() plot(). subpopulation effects, cate() cqte() functions compute conditional contrasts user-specified covariate rows. following code produces conditional quantile survival predictions grid points, returning treated-minus-control contrasts quantiles arm-specific summaries survival.
code: data("causal_pos500_p3_k2", package = "CausalMixGPD") overview_causal_dat <- causal_pos500_p3_k2 overview_causal_df  <- data.frame(   y = overview_causal_dat$y,   A = overview_causal_dat$A,   overview_causal_dat$X ) overview_causal_fit <- dpmgpd.causal(   formula = y ~ x1 + x2 + x3,   data = overview_causal_df,   treat = "A",   backend = "sb",   kernel = "lognormal",   components = 5,   PS = "logit",   ps_scale = "logit",   ps_summary = "mean",   mcmc_outcome = mcmc_fixed,   mcmc_ps = mcmc_fixed ) overview_att <- att(overview_causal_fit, level = 0.90, interval = "credible") overview_qtt <- qtt(   overview_causal_fit,   probs = c(0.50, 0.90, 0.95),   level = 0.90,   interval = "hpd" ) summary(overview_att) summary(overview_qtt) plot(overview_att, type = "effect") plot(overview_qtt, type = "effect") qs    <- c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75) overview_causal_xgrid <- expand.grid(lapply(   overview_causal_df[c("x1", "x2", "x3")],   quantile,   probs = qs )) overview_pred_q <- predict(   overview_causal_fit,   newdata = overview_causal_xgrid,   type = "quantile",   p = c(0.50, 0.90) ) overview_pred_s <- predict(   overview_causal_fit,   newdata = overview_causal_xgrid,   type = "survival",   y = rep(4, nrow(overview_causal_xgrid)) )

title: One-Arm Modeling with CausalMixGPD
text: upper tail requires explicit modeling, package replaces part density threshold u(x)u(x) GPD component. exceedances threshold, define Z=Y−u(x)given Y>u(x). Z = Y - u(x) \quad \text{given } Y > u(x). peaks--threshold framework, conditional excess distribution approximated GPD threshold sufficiently high (Balkema Haan 1974; Pickands 1975; Coles 2001). y>u(x)y > u(x), scale parameter σ(x)>0\sigma(x) > 0 shape parameter ξ\xi, GPD cumulative distribution function FGPD(y∣u(x),σ(x),ξ)={1−(1+ξy−u(x)σ(x))−1/ξ,ξ≠0,1−exp⁡(−y−u(x)σ(x)),ξ=0. F_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y \mid u(x), \sigma(x), \xi) = \begin{cases} 1 - \left(1 + \xi \frac{y-u(x)}{\sigma(x)}\right)^{-1/\xi}, & \xi \ne 0, \\ 1 - \exp\left(-\frac{y-u(x)}{\sigma(x)}\right), & \xi = 0. \end{cases} support y≥u(x)y \ge u(x) ξ≥0\xi \ge 0, u(x)≤y≤u(x)−σ(x)/ξu(x) \le y \le u(x) - \sigma(x)/\xi ξ<0\xi < 0. shape parameter controls tail regime: negative values imply finite upper endpoint, ξ=0\xi = 0 gives exponential-type tail, positive values correspond heavy tails.

title: One-Arm Modeling with CausalMixGPD
text: key statistical idea package spliced model. Let FDP(⋅∣x;Θ)F_{\mathrm{DP}}(\cdot \mid x; \Theta) fDP(⋅∣x;Θ)f_{\mathrm{DP}}(\cdot \mid x; \Theta) denote bulk CDF density, let Φ\Phi collect tail parameters. Write pu(x;Θ)=FDP(u(x)∣x;Θ) p_u(x; \Theta) = F_{\mathrm{DP}}(u(x) \mid x; \Theta) bulk probability mass threshold. spliced conditional CDF F(y∣x;Θ,Φ)={FDP(y∣x;Θ),y≤u(x),pu(x;Θ)+{1−pu(x;Θ)}FGPD(y∣x;Φ),y>u(x), F(y \mid x; \Theta, \Phi) = \begin{cases} F_{\mathrm{DP}}(y \mid x; \Theta), & y \le u(x), \\ p_u(x; \Theta) + \{1 - p_u(x; \Theta)\} F_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y \mid x; \Phi), & y > u(x), \end{cases} corresponding conditional density f(y∣x;Θ,Φ)={fDP(y∣x;Θ),y≤u(x),{1−pu(x;Θ)}fGPD(y∣x;Φ),y>u(x). f(y \mid x; \Theta, \Phi) = \begin{cases} f_{\mathrm{DP}}(y \mid x; \Theta), & y \le u(x), \\ \{1 - p_u(x; \Theta)\} f_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y \mid x; \Phi), & y > u(x). \end{cases} formulation preserves flexibility mixture central region forcing tail obey EVT-motivated form. splice continuous u(x)u(x) construction, tail density normalized bulk survival threshold. quantile function follows two-part logic. quantile levels threshold mass, package inverts bulk CDF. levels threshold mass, probability index rescaled exceedance scale: τ̃(x;Θ)=τ−pu(x;Θ)1−pu(x;Θ). \widetilde\tau(x; \Theta) = \frac{\tau - p_u(x; \Theta)}{1 - p_u(x; \Theta)}. upper-tail quantiles obtained GPD quantile function evaluated τ̃(x;Θ)\widetilde\tau(x; \Theta). distinction explains upper quantile predictions one main places spliced fit can differ meaningfully bulk-fit.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Fit a spliced CRP model data("nc_posX100_p3_k2", package = "CausalMixGPD") fit <- dpmgpd(y ~ x1 + x2 + x3, data = nc_posX100_p3_k2,               backend = "spliced", kernel = "lognormal",               components = 4, GPD = TRUE)  # Extract allocation alloc <- allocation(fit) print(alloc) summary(alloc) plot(alloc)  # Predict for new data X_new <- data.frame(x1 = rnorm(20), x2 = rnorm(20), x3 = rnorm(20)) y_new <- rlnorm(20, meanlog = 1, sdlog = 0.5) newdata <- cbind(y = y_new, X_new) alloc_new <- allocation(fit, newdata = newdata) plot(alloc_new, overlay = TRUE) } # }

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: Writing loc = , scale = theta, shape1 = alpha, shape2 = beta, transformed quantity \(((X - ) / \theta)^\beta\) follows gamma law shape \(\alpha\). uppercase NIMBLE-compatible functions scalar (x/q n = 1). vectorized R usage, use base_lowercase(). Amoroso family used package defined density $$ f(x) = \left|\frac{\beta}{\theta}\right| \frac{z^{\alpha \beta - 1}\exp(-z^\beta)}{\Gamma(\alpha)}, \qquad z = \frac{x-}{\theta}, $$ side location parameter determined sign \(\theta\). Equivalently, \(Z = ((X-)/\theta)^\beta\) follows Gamma distribution shape \(\alpha\) unit scale. representation explains quantile function computed gamma quantile mapped back inverse transformation. mean exists whenever \(\alpha + 1/\beta\) lies domain gamma function used moment formula. package family serves flexible positive-support bulk kernel capable reproducing gamma-like, Weibull-like, skewed shapes single parameterization.

title: Amoroso mixture distribution — amoroso_mix
text: mixture density $$ f(x) = \sum_{k = 1}^K \tilde{w}_k f_{Amoroso}(x \mid a_k, \theta_k, \alpha_k, \beta_k), $$ normalized weights \(\tilde{w}_k\). scalar functions NIMBLE-compatible; vectorized R usage, use amoroso_lowercase(). Amoroso family especially useful positive-support data can reproduce wide range skewed heavy-right-tail shapes remaining analytically tractable gamma transformation. mixture CDF $$ F(x) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k F_{Amoroso}(x \mid a_k,\theta_k,\alpha_k,\beta_k), $$ random generation proceeds selecting component sampling component. Closed-form mixture quantiles available, qAmorosoMix() inverts mixture CDF numerically. analytical mixture mean weighted average component means, \(a_k + \theta_k \Gamma(\alpha_k + 1/\beta_k) / \Gamma(\alpha_k)\), whenever component moments exist.

what: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
text: Spliced bulk-tail family formed attaching generalized Pareto tail Amoroso mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) denote Amoroso mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \left\{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\right\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) GPD CDF exceedances threshold.

title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
text: density, CDF, RNG implemented nimbleFunctions use NIMBLE models. quantile function R function uses numerical inversion bulk region closed-form GPD quantile tail region. Amoroso mixture describes bulk threshold generalized Pareto describes exceedances . \(F_{mix}(u)=p_u\), splice uses $$ f(x) = \left\{ \begin{array}{ll} f_{mix}(x), & x < u, \\ (1-p_u) g_{GPD}(x \mid u,\sigma_u,\xi), & x \ge u. \end{array} \right. $$ Bulk quantiles computed numerically Amoroso mixture CDF tail quantiles obtained GPD inverse rescaling tail probability.

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
code: build_causal_bundle(   y,   X,   A,   backend = c("sb", "crp", "spliced"),   kernel,   GPD = FALSE,   components = NULL,   param_specs = NULL,   mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   mcmc_ps = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   epsilon = 0.025,   alpha_random = TRUE,   ps_prior = list(mean = 0, sd = 2),   include_intercept = TRUE,   PS = "logit",   ps_scale = c("logit", "prob"),   ps_summary = c("mean", "median"),   ps_clamp = 1e-06,   monitor = c("core", "full"),   monitor_latent = FALSE,   monitor_v = FALSE )

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: y Numeric outcome vector. X Design matrix data.frame covariates (N x P). Binary treatment indicator (length N, values 0/1). backend Character; Dirichlet process representation outcome models: "sb": stick-breaking truncation "crp": Chinese Restaurant Process "spliced": CRP GPD tail splicing length 2, first entry used treated (=1) second control (=0). kernel Character kernel name outcome models (must exist get_kernel_registry()). length 2: first entry: used treated (=1) second entry: used control (=0) GPD Logical; include GPD tail outcomes TRUE. length 2: first entry: used treated (=1) second entry: used control (=0) components Integer >= 2; truncation parameter outcome mixtures. length 2: first entry: used treated (=1) second entry: used control (=0) param_specs Outcome parameter overrides (structure build_nimble_bundle()): single list: used arms list con trt entries: arm-specific overrides mcmc_outcome MCMC settings list outcome bundles. mcmc_ps MCMC settings list PS model. epsilon Numeric [0,1) used outcome bundles posterior truncation summaries. length 2: first entry: used treated (=1) second entry: used control (=0) alpha_random Logical; whether outcome-model DP concentration parameter \(\kappa\) stochastic. ps_prior Normal prior PS coefficients. List mean sd. include_intercept Logical; TRUE, intercept column prepended X PS model. PS Character logical; controls propensity score estimation: "logit" (default): Logistic regression PS model "probit": Probit regression PS model "naive": Gaussian naive Bayes PS model FALSE: PS estimation; outcome models use X PS model choice stored bundle metadata downstream use prediction summaries, enabling seamless integration future PS estimation methods. ps_scale Scale used augmenting outcomes PS: "logit": augment logit (log-odds) scale "prob": augment probability scale ps_summary Posterior summary PS: "mean": posterior mean propensity scores "median": posterior median propensity scores ps_clamp Numeric epsilon clamping PS values \((\epsilon, 1-\epsilon)\). monitor Character monitor profile: "core" (default): monitors essential model parameters "full": monitors model nodes monitor_latent Logical; whether monitor latent cluster labels (z) outcome arms. monitor_v Logical; whether monitor stick-breaking v terms SB outcomes.

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: list class "causalmixgpd_causal_bundle" containing design bundle, two outcome bundles, training data, arm indices, metadata required posterior prediction causal effect summaries.

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: outcome bundles reuse one-arm DPM plus optional GPD machinery. PS block provides shared adjustment object used run_mcmc_causal predict.causalmixgpd_causal_fit. causal bundle encodes two arm-specific predictive laws \(F_0(y \mid x)\) \(F_1(y \mid x)\). Downstream causal estimands functionals two distributions: $$\mathrm{ATE} = E\{Y(1)\} - E\{Y(0)\}, \qquad \mathrm{QTE}(\tau) = Q_1(\tau) - Q_0(\tau).$$ PS enabled, package estimates propensity score model \(e(x) = \Pr(= 1 \mid X = x)\) uses posterior summary score augmented covariate arm-specific outcome models. mirrors workflow described manuscript vignette.

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) N <- 100 X <- cbind(x1 = rnorm(N), x2 = runif(N)) A <- rbinom(N, 1, plogis(0.3 + 0.5 * X[, 1])) y <- rexp(N) + 0.1  cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   A = A,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "gamma",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 10,   PS = "probit" ) } # }

code: build_nimble_bundle(   y,   X = NULL,   ps = NULL,   backend = c("sb", "crp", "spliced"),   kernel,   GPD = FALSE,   components = NULL,   param_specs = NULL,   mcmc = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   epsilon = 0.025,   alpha_random = TRUE,   monitor = c("core", "full"),   monitor_latent = FALSE,   monitor_v = FALSE )

title: Build the explicit one-arm NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: y Numeric outcome vector. X Optional design matrix/data.frame (N x p) conditional variants. ps Optional numeric vector (length N) propensity scores. provided, augments design matrix PS-adjusted outcome modeling. backend Character; Dirichlet process representation: "sb": stick-breaking truncation "crp": Chinese Restaurant Process kernel Character kernel name (must exist get_kernel_registry()). GPD Logical; whether GPD tail requested. components Integer >= 2. Single user-facing truncation parameter: SB: number mixture components used stick-breaking truncation CRP: maximum number clusters represented finite NIMBLE model param_specs Optional list entries bulk tail override defaults. mcmc Named list MCMC settings (niter, nburnin, thin, nchains, seed). Stored bundle. epsilon Numeric [0,1). downstream summaries/plots/prediction keep smaller k defined either () cumulative mass >= 1 - epsilon (ii) per-component weights >= epsilon, renormalize. alpha_random Logical; whether DP concentration parameter \(\kappa\) stochastic. monitor Character monitor profile: "core" (default): monitors essential model parameters "full": monitors model nodes monitor_latent Logical; TRUE, include latent cluster labels (z) monitors. monitor_v Logical; TRUE backend SB, include stick breaks (v) monitors.

title: Build the explicit one-arm NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle

title: Build the explicit one-arm NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: returned bundle encodes finite approximation Dirichlet process mixture using either stick-breaking ("sb") Chinese restaurant process / spliced ("crp" / "spliced") representation. bulk-model, target likelihood DPM predictive law $$f(y \mid x) = \sum_{k=1}^{K} w_k(x) f_k(y \mid x, \theta_k).$$ GPD = TRUE, bundle augments bulk model threshold \(u(x)\) generalized Pareto tail threshold, producing spliced predictive distribution described manuscript vignette. function intentionally stops model compilation sampling. Use run_mcmc_bundle_manual mcmc execute stored model definition. object contains: compiled model spec nimbleCode model code constants, data, explicit dimensions initialization function inits (stored function) monitor specification MCMC settings list (stored used code generation)

title: Build the workflow bundle used by the package fitters — bundle
text: one-arm models returned object represents bulk Dirichlet process mixture, optionally augmented spliced generalized Pareto tail. causal models returned object contains two arm-specific outcome bundles plus optional propensity score block. workflow : prepare bundle bundle(), run posterior sampling mcmc one dpmix*/dpmgpd* wrappers, inspect fitted object summary.mixgpd_fit, params, predict.mixgpd_fit, causal estimand helpers. Setting GPD = TRUE requests spliced bulk-tail model conditional distribution $$F(y \mid x) = F_{\mathrm{bulk}}(y \mid x)\mathbf{1}\{y \le u(x)\} + \left[p_u(x) + \{1 - p_u(x)\}F_{\mathrm{GPD}}(y \mid x)\right]\mathbf{1}\{y > u(x)\},$$ \(p_u(x)\) bulk probability threshold \(u(x)\). See manuscript vignette DPM hierarchy, SB/CRP representations, spliced bulk-tail construction used throughout package.

Cauchy law stable heavy-tailed distribution undefined mean variance. package allows Cauchy kernel bulk distribution deliberately pair GPD tails kernel registry. predictive summaries, ordinary means available Cauchy kernels; tail-robust summaries medians, quantiles, survival curves, restricted means remain well defined. distribution function $$ F(x) = \frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{\pi}\arctan\left(\frac{x-\ell}{s}\right), $$ quantile corresponding inverse $$ Q(p) = \ell + s \tan\{\pi(p-1/2)\}. $$

title: Gamma mixture distribution — gamma_mix
text: mixture density $$ f(x) = \sum_{k = 1}^K \tilde{w}_k f_{\Gamma}(x \mid \alpha_k, \theta_k), \qquad x > 0, $$ normalized weights \(\tilde{w}_k\). vectorized R usage, use gamma_lowercase(). package parameterization, component density \(f_\Gamma(x \mid \alpha,\theta) = x^{\alpha-1}\exp(-x/\theta) / \{\Gamma(\alpha)\theta^\alpha\}\) \(x>0\). mixture CDF therefore $$ F(x) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k F_\Gamma(x \mid \alpha_k,\theta_k). $$ Random generation first selects component according normalized mixture weights draws corresponding gamma distribution. Since finite gamma mixtures closed form quantiles, qGammaMix() obtains numerically inverting mixture CDF. analytical mean $$ E(X) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k \alpha_k \theta_k. $$ expression reused posterior predictive mean calculations gamma-based fits.

title: Generalized Pareto distribution — gpd
text: parameterization $$ G(x) = 1 - \left(1 + \xi \frac{x - u}{\sigma_u}\right)^{-1 / \xi}, \qquad x \ge u, $$ threshold = u, scale = sigma_u > 0, shape = xi. shape approaches zero, distribution reduces exponential tail limit. uppercase NIMBLE-compatible functions scalar (x/q n = 1). vectorized R usage, use base_lowercase(). associated density $$ g(x) = \frac{1}{\sigma_u} \left(1 + \xi \frac{x - u}{\sigma_u}\right)^{-1/\xi - 1}, $$ support \(1 + \xi (x-u)/\sigma_u > 0\). \(\xi = 0\), reduces exponential density mean excess \(\sigma_u\). GPD finite mean \(\xi < 1\), finite variance \(\xi < 1/2\). existence conditions matter downstream predictive means package: spliced models \(\xi \ge 1\) require restricted means rather ordinary means. bulk distribution CDF \(F_{bulk}\), package's spliced families use tail construction $$ F(x) = \left\{ \begin{array}{ll} F_{bulk}(x), & x < u, \\ F_{bulk}(u) + \{1 - F_{bulk}(u)\} G(x), & x \ge u. \end{array} \right. $$

title: Laplace (double exponential) mixture distribution — laplace_mix
text: mixture density $$ f(x) = \sum_{k = 1}^K \tilde{w}_k f_{Lap}(x \mid \mu_k, b_k), $$ normalized weights \(\tilde{w}_k\). vectorized R usage, use laplace_lowercase(). component Laplace law density \(f(x \mid \mu,b) = (2b)^{-1}\exp\{-|x-\mu|/b\}\). mixture CDF corresponding weighted average component CDFs, random generation selects component first samples component. finite mixtures package, quantile closed form therefore obtained numerically. analytical mean mixture simply $$ E(X) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k \mu_k. $$ formula used downstream predictive mean calculations Laplace-based fits.

title: Lognormal mixture distribution — lognormal_mix
text: mixture density $$ f(x) = \sum_{k = 1}^K \tilde{w}_k f_{LN}(x \mid \mu_k, \sigma_k), \qquad x > 0, $$ normalized weights \(\tilde{w}_k\). vectorized R usage, use lognormal_lowercase(). component satisfies \(\log X_k \sim N(\mu_k,\sigma_k^2)\), mixture CDF $$ F(x) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k \Phi\left(\frac{\log x-\mu_k}{\sigma_k}\right), \qquad x>0. $$ Random generation proceeds drawing component index probability \(\tilde{w}_k\) sampling corresponding lognormal law. finite mixture lognormals admit closed-form inverse CDF, qLognormalMix() computes quantiles numerical inversion. analytical mixture mean $$ E(X) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k \exp(\mu_k + \sigma_k^2/2), $$ expression used package whenever ordinary predictive mean exists.

title: Lognormal mixture with a GPD tail — lognormal_mixgpd
text: density, CDF, RNG implemented nimbleFunctions. quantile R function: uses numerical inversion bulk region closed-form GPD quantile tail. Let \(F_{mix}\) lognormal-mixture CDF let \(u\) denote threshold. splice uses bulk law \(u\) attaches GPD residual survival mass \(u\). density therefore becomes $$ f(x) = \left\{ \begin{array}{ll} f_{mix}(x), & x < u, \\ \{1-F_{mix}(u)\} g_{GPD}(x \mid u,\sigma_u,\xi), & x \ge u. \end{array} \right. $$ quantile computed piecewise: bulk quantiles obtained numerically mixture CDF, whereas tail quantiles use closed-form GPD inverse rescaling upper-tail probability.

title: Normal mixture distribution — normal_mix
text: weights \(w_k\), means \(\mu_k\), standard deviations \(\sigma_k\), mixture density $$ f(x) = \sum_{k = 1}^K \tilde{w}_k \phi(x \mid \mu_k, \sigma_k^2), $$ \(\tilde{w}_k = w_k / \sum_j w_j\). uppercase functions scalar NIMBLE-compatible building blocks. vectorized R usage, use normal_lowercase(). \(F_k\) denotes \(k\)-th component CDF, mixture distribution function $$ F(x) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k F_k(x) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k \Phi\left(\frac{x-\mu_k}{\sigma_k}\right). $$ Random generation first draws component index probability \(\tilde{w}_k\) generates corresponding normal law. quantile function closed form general finite mixture, qNormMix() solves \(F(x)=p\) numerically bracketing root applying stats::uniroot(). mixture mean $$ E(X) = \sum_{k=1}^K \tilde{w}_k \mu_k, $$ analytical mean used package normal-mixture draw contributes posterior predictive mean calculation.

title: Normal mixture with a GPD tail — normal_mixgpd
text: \(F_{mix}\) denotes normal-mixture CDF, spliced CDF $$ F(x) = \left\{ \begin{array}{ll} F_{mix}(x), & x < u, \\ F_{mix}(u) + \{1 - F_{mix}(u)\} G(x), & x \ge u, \end{array} \right. $$ threshold = u \(G\) GPD CDF. construction keeps normal mixture unchanged threshold \(u\) replaces upper tail generalized Pareto exceedance model. Writing \(F_{mix}(u)=p_u\), spliced density $$ f(x) = \left\{ \begin{array}{ll} f_{mix}(x), & x < u, \\ \{1-p_u\} g_{GPD}(x \mid u,\sigma_u,\xi), & x \ge u. \end{array} \right. $$ formulation preserves total probability GPD attached residual survival mass bulk threshold. quantile piecewise. \(p \le p_u\), qNormMixGpd() inverts bulk mixture CDF; otherwise rescales tail probability \((p-p_u)/(1-p_u)\) applies closed-form GPD quantile. piecewise logic fitted-model prediction code uses draw draw.

Used model fit propensity score augmentation. id Optional identifier prediction rows. Provide either column name newdata vector length nrow(newdata). id column excluded analysis. type Prediction type: "density": Posterior predictive density f(y | x, data) "survival": Posterior predictive survival S(y | x, data) = 1 - F(y | x, data) "quantile": Posterior predictive quantiles Q(p | x, data) "sample": Posterior predictive samples Y^rep ~ f(y | x, data) "mean": Posterior predictive mean E(Y | x, data) (averaged posterior parameter uncertainty) "rmean": Posterior predictive restricted mean \(E[\min(Y, cutoff) \mid x, data]\) "median": Posterior predictive median (quantile p=0.5) "fit": Per-observation posterior predictive draws Note: type="mean" returns posterior predictive mean, integrates parameter uncertainty. differs mean single model distribution. p Numeric vector probabilities quantiles (required type="quantile"). index Alias p; numeric vector quantile levels. nsim Number posterior predictive samples (type="sample"). level Credible level credible intervals (default 0.95 95 percent intervals). interval Character NULL; type credible interval: NULL: interval "credible" (default): equal-tailed quantile intervals "hpd": highest posterior density intervals probs Quantiles credible interval bands. store_draws Logical; whether store posterior draws (type="sample"). nsim_mean Number posterior predictive samples used simulation-based mean targets. Ignored analytical type = "mean"; still used type = "rmean". cutoff Finite numeric cutoff type="rmean" (restricted mean). ncores Number CPU cores use parallel prediction (supported). show_progress Logical; TRUE, print step messages render progress supported. ndraws_pred Optional integer subsample posterior draws prediction speed. NULL nrow(newdata) > 20000, defaults 200. chunk_size Optional row chunk size large newdata prediction. NULL nrow(newdata) > 20000, defaults 10000. parallel Logical; TRUE, enable parallel prediction (alias setting ncores > 1). workers Optional integer worker count (alias ncores). ... Unused.

title: Posterior predictive summaries from a fitted one-arm model — predict.mixgpd_fit
text: list elements: fit: numeric vector/matrix type = "sample", otherwise data frame estimate/lower/upper columns (posterior means draws) plus index columns (e.g. id, y, index). fit_df: machine-readable data frame view prediction output. non-sample types aliases fit; type = "sample" long-form data frame draw indices sampled values. lower, upper: reserved backward compatibility (typically NULL). type, grid: metadata.

title: Posterior predictive summaries from a fitted one-arm model — predict.mixgpd_fit
text: method works posterior predictive functionals rather raw model parameters. Supported output types include: "density" \(f(y \mid x)\), "survival" \(S(y \mid x) = 1 - F(y \mid x)\), "quantile" \(Q(\tau \mid x)\), "mean" \(E(Y \mid x)\), "rmean" \(E\{\min(Y, c) \mid x\}\), "sample" "fit" draw-level predictive output. spliced models predictions integrate DPM bulk GPD tail using component-specific tail parameters, including link-mode tail coefficients present. kernels finite analytical mean, type = "mean" computes posterior-draw mean analytically summarizes draw-level means across posterior. type = "rmean" path remains separate posterior predictive simulation pipeline. kernels analytical mean, type = "mean" computed analytically within posterior draw summarized draws. GPD-tail fits analytical path used tail shape parameter satisfies \(\xi < 1\). mean exist analytically chosen kernel required GPD tail \(\xi \ge 1\), ordinary mean undefined function errors message directing type = "rmean" tail-robust summaries.

title: Posterior predictive summaries from a fitted one-arm model — predict.mixgpd_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal",                              GPD = TRUE, components = 6,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) pr <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9)) pr_surv <- predict(fit, y = sort(y), type = "survival") pr_cdf <- list(fit = 1 - pr_surv$fit) # HPD intervals pr_hpd <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = "hpd") # No intervals pr_none <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = NULL) # Restricted mean (finite under heavy tails) pr_rmean <- predict(fit, type = "rmean", cutoff = 10, interval = "credible") } # }

title: Residual diagnostics on the training design — residuals.mixgpd_fit
text: object fitted object class "mixgpd_fit" (must covariates). type Residual type: "raw": observed minus fitted values "pit": probability integral transform residuals (see pit argument) fitted_type type = "raw", use fitted means medians. pit PIT mode type = "pit": "plugin": plug-PIT using posterior mean CDF. "bayes_mean": Bayesian PIT using draw-wise CDFs averaged draws. "bayes_draw": Bayesian PIT using single draw-wise CDF per observation. Bayesian PIT modes drop invalid posterior draws using validation rules prediction attach diagnostics via attr(res, "pit_diagnostics"). pit_seed Optional integer seed reproducible bayes_draw sampling. ... Unused.

PIT residuals instead assess calibration posterior predictive CDF. plug-PIT uses posterior mean CDF, Bayesian PIT variants work draw draw. method available unconditional models training design matrix stored observation-specific fitted values.

title: Residual diagnostics on the training design — residuals.mixgpd_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 X <- data.frame(x1 = stats::rnorm(50), x2 = stats::runif(50)) bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, X = X, backend = "sb", kernel = "lognormal",                              GPD = FALSE, components = 4,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) pit_plugin <- residuals(fit, type = "pit", pit = "plugin") pit_bayes_mean <- residuals(fit, type = "pit", pit = "bayes_mean", pit_seed = 1L) pit_bayes_draw <- residuals(fit, type = "pit", pit = "bayes_draw", pit_seed = 1L) attr(pit_bayes_draw, "pit_diagnostics") } # }
